other_observers: Donna Emmons, Sandra Mcnew, Richard and Mary Tucey; David Silverman and above observers
description: A solid white heron excepting dark legs and feet, and dusky dagger shaped bill becoming darker at the tip, this bird was larger than nearby Little Blue Herons and smaller than nearby Great Blue Herons. The bird was slender, appearing longer-necked and longer legged than Little Blue Herons. Due to distance we were not able to determine the exact colors of the bill.Behaviors: Often doing dancing staggering side step with one leg crossing over the other like a tipsy reveler along a sandy lakeshore. Sometimes it jumped up & down with wings spread.Call: none heardPlumage: possibly 2nd cycle or adult
